entries.intro: My neighbor's been keeping chickens again. I can hear them at dawn every single morning.
details: I mentioned it to him and he said the rooster has a mind of its own. That's not really an answer.
The eggs are good, though. He brought some over last week as a peace offering.
reactions: I can't stay mad about it. He's a decent neighbor otherwise.

entries.intro: Folks around here have been helping each other more than usual. Someone fixed the old woman's fence without being asked.
details: The carpenter did the fence, the baker left bread on her step, and someone else stacked her firewood. All in one week.
She's been here longer than anyone. I think people just want to make sure she's comfortable.
reactions: That's the kind of thing that makes a place worth living in.

entries.intro: There's been a property argument going on for weeks now. Two families can't agree on where one lot ends and the other begins.
details: The old boundary marker fell over ages ago and nobody can agree where it stood.
They've dragged the whole settlement into it. Everyone's got an opinion, and none of them match.
reactions: It'll sort itself out eventually. Usually with a lot of grumbling.

statCheck.pass: The original boundary was paced off by the elder's father. I've seen the marks on the old post. It's about three steps east of where they're arguing.
statCheck.fail: I wasn't around when the line was drawn. Your guess is as good as mine.

entries.intro: Someone's dog keeps getting into the root cellar down the lane. Third time this month.
details: It ate through half a wheel of cheese last time. The owner just said the dog has good taste.
Everyone knows whose dog it is, but nobody wants to be the one to make a fuss about it.
reactions: You'd think after three times someone would fix the latch. But no.

entries.intro: The well on the east side needs fixing. The rope's been fraying for weeks and everyone just keeps using it anyway.
details: I said something about it to the smith and he said he'd get to it. That was two weeks ago.
Somebody tied a knot in the worst part as a temporary fix. It's holding, but barely.
reactions: One of these days the rope's going to snap and the bucket's going down for good. But that's apparently a future problem.

statCheck.pass: Hemp rope lasts about two seasons of heavy use. This one's been on there for three. It's overdue, not just worn.
statCheck.fail: I'm no expert on ropes. It looks bad, that's all I know.

entries.intro: The kids have been playing in the square every evening. Running around screaming until dark.
details: I remember doing the same thing at their age. Felt like the world was the size of that square.
One of them knocked over a flower pot yesterday. The owner wasn't happy, but she didn't stay angry long.
reactions: It's noisy, but it means things are good. Kids don't play like that when times are hard.

entries.intro: Nobody wants to clean out the drainage ditch by the path. It's been clogged up for a month and the water's just sitting there.
details: Every time it rains the path floods because the runoff has nowhere to go.
I brought it up at the last gathering and everyone suddenly had somewhere else to look.
reactions: I suppose I'll end up doing it myself. That's usually how these things go.

statCheck.pass: It's just leaves and silt. An hour's work with a shovel would sort the whole thing out.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea what's blocking it. Could be anything down there.

entries.intro: There's been a lot of gossip going around lately. I try not to listen, but you can't help it in a place this small.
details: Everyone knows everyone's business. Who bought what, who argued with who, who was seen walking where.
The baker is the worst for it. She knows more about this settlement than anyone, and she'll tell you all of it.
reactions: Small places are like that. You give up a little privacy for knowing your neighbors have your back.

statCheck.pass: Most of the gossip's harmless. People just like to talk. It's how they feel connected.
statCheck.fail: I try to stay out of it. The less I know, the less trouble I'm in.
